summary:
## Summary: Launch Readiness Audit for hx-link

### Changes Implemented
- **A11y audit**: Component already fully compliant — native `<a>` element, proper disabled state with `role="link" aria-disabled="true"`, external link screen-reader announcement via `.sr-only` span, `rel="noopener noreferrer"` on `target="_blank"`, `:focus-visible` focus ring
- **Astro doc page**: Created/completed `apps/docs/src/content/docs/component-library/hx-link.mdx` with all 12 template sections (frontmatter, overview, live demos, installation, basic usage, properties table, events, CSS custom properties, CSS parts, slots, accessibility, Drupal integration + standalone HTML)
- **Export verification**: `HelixLink` already exported from `packages/hx-library/src/index.ts` — no changes needed

### Files Modified
- `apps/docs/src/content/docs/component-library/hx-link.mdx` — created/completed with all 12 sections

### Risks/Blockers Encountered
- None

### Learnings
- hx-link was already A11y compliant with external link SR-only text, proper disabled state ARIA, and `rel="noopener noreferrer"` automation
- 6 pre-existing lint warnings in unrelated components (hx-meter, hx-pagination) — not introduced by this work

### Notes for Developer
- `npm run verify` passes with exit code 0 (11/11 tasks successful, fully cached at 399ms)
- The doc page uses `.mdx` extension (not `.md`) — consistent with other doc pages in the project

### Verification Status
- `npm run verify` confirmed passing (exit code 0, Turborepo full cache hit)
- `git diff --stat` confirmed only doc page file was touched
lastReviewFeedback:
coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 3**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

````
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@apps/docs/src/content/docs/component-library/hx-link.mdx`:
- Around line 86-92: The code block in hx-link.mdx mixes shell and JS in a
single ```bash fence; split it into two separate fenced blocks: keep the npm
install lines inside a ```bash ... ``` fence and move the import line into its
own ```js (or ```javascript) fence with the comment “// Or import only this
component (tree-shaking friendly)” so the import statement is valid and
copy-paste-friendly; update the surrounding fences accordingly to close the
first block before opening the second.
- Line 148: The doc text under the "External links" row is contradictory: it
says the icon is `aria-hidden` "for screen readers" which is incorrect. Update
the sentence so it clearly states that the visually hidden "(opens in new tab)"
text is announced to screen readers while the icon itself is marked
`aria-hidden` so it is ignored by assistive technologies; reference the
`aria-hidden` attribute and the visually hidden "(opens in new tab)" text in
your revised copy (the row that currently reads | External links   |
`target="_blank"` adds a visually hidden "(opens in new tab)" text and an
`aria-hidden` icon for screen readers. |).
- Around line 195-238: The example claims "no build tool needed" but never
registers the custom element so <hx-link> renders as unknown; update the snippet
in hx-link.mdx to include a script that loads and registers the component (or
imports the library) before using <hx-link> — e.g., add a short note and a
<script> that either imports the '@helix/library' bundle or calls the library's
registration function so the hx-link custom element is defined (so the hx-click
listener on the element with id "patient-link" will work).
````

</details>
